 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-l9wf","title":"TEST: FCPS/FCPC Protocol Unit Tests (Frame Parsing, AEAD, MAC Verification)","description":"# TEST: FCPS/FCPC Protocol Unit Tests


## Goal
Prove FCPS/FCPC framing, parsing, and crypto verification are correct and bounded under adversarial inputs.

## Scope
Comprehensive unit tests for wire protocol frame parsing and cryptographic verification.

## FCPS (Symbol Plane) Tests

### Frame Parsing
- [ ] Valid frame parses correctly
- [ ] Magic bytes validation ("FCPS")
- [ ] Version field bounds checking
- [ ] Flag combinations parsed correctly
- [ ] Symbol count matches actual symbols
- [ ] Total payload length matches content
- [ ] Zone ID hash is 32 bytes exactly
- [ ] Object ID is 32 bytes exactly

### Symbol Record Parsing
- [ ] ESI (encoding symbol identifier) extraction
- [ ] K parameter extraction
- [ ] Data length matches symbol_size
- [ ] Auth tag is 16 bytes exactly

### Datagram Envelope
- [ ] Session ID extraction (16 bytes)
- [ ] Sequence number parsing (u64 LE)
- [ ] MAC extraction (16 bytes)
- [ ] MAC verification success with correct key
- [ ] MAC verification failure with wrong key
- [ ] MAC verification failure with tampered frame
- [ ] Direction byte in MAC context

### Per-Symbol AEAD
- [ ] Sender key derivation matches spec
- [ ] Nonce derivation: frame_seq || esi
- [ ] AAD binding includes all required fields
- [ ] Decrypt success with correct key
- [ ] Decrypt failure with wrong key
- [ ] Decrypt failure with tampered ciphertext
- [ ] Decrypt failure with wrong AAD

### Frame Flags
- [ ] REQUIRES_ACK flag handling
- [ ] COMPRESSED flag handling
- [ ] ENCRYPTED flag handling
- [ ] STREAMING + STREAM_END combination
- [ ] HAS_CAP_TOKEN flag handling
- [ ] ZONE_CROSSING flag handling
- [ ] CONTROL_PLANE flag handling
- [ ] Invalid flag combinations rejected

## FCPC (Control Plane) Tests

### Frame Structure
- [ ] Canonical CBOR envelope parsing
- [ ] Object type discrimination
- [ ] Length prefix validation
- [ ] Backpressure frame parsing

### Control Objects
- [ ] ControlPlaneObject type dispatch
- [ ] Session control messages
- [ ] Capability objects
- [ ] Revocation objects
- [ ] Checkpoint objects

## DoS Resistance Tests
- [ ] Oversized frame rejected
- [ ] Invalid symbol count rejected
- [ ] Frame exceeding MTU rejected
- [ ] Memory bounds on malformed input

## Golden Vectors
Each test category needs:
- Valid frame bytes (hex)
- Expected parsed structure
- Invalid frame bytes that MUST be rejected

## Fuzz Targets
- FCPS frame parsing
- FCPC frame parsing
- Symbol record parsing
- Datagram envelope parsing

## Logging Requirements
```json
{
  "test_name": "...",
  "protocol": "fcps|fcpc",
  "operation": "parse|verify|encode",
  "frame_size": 1234,
  "result": "pass|fail",
  "error_code": "optional"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All parsing paths have test coverage
- DoS protections verified
- Golden vectors match exactly
- Fuzz targets find no crashes

","notes":"Tests validated: all 72 fcp-protocol tests pass. Coverage confirmed for: FCPS frame parsing/flags/symbols, FCPC framing/AEAD/replay, MAC verification, golden vectors. Fuzz targets exist for all components. Blocked waiting for 1n78.13 completion.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WindySnow","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T07:55:24.993533714Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T08:30:23.929675939Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T08:30:23.929675939Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added comprehensive FCPS/FCPC protocol unit tests including DoS resistance (symbol count overflow, MTU violation, memory bounds), replay protection, and reference vector generation. All 50 golden vector tests pass (28 FCPC + 22 FCPS). Implementation dependency (1n78.13) is still in progress but tests are self-contained and complete.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp2","mvp","protocol","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-l9wf","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-l9wf","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.13","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-l9wf","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-la0l1","title":"fcp-audit: AuditEntry has no signer binding (authenticity gap at crate boundary)","description":"## Finding
`AuditEntry` (crates/fcp-audit/src/lib.rs:152) carries `actor: String` but has no signer key, no signature, no issuer attestation field. `computed_id` (lib.rs:234) is a blake3 hash of the canonical payload (integrity-against-edit, not authenticity). `verify_chain` performs no signer check.

Consequences:
1. **Forgery by any entity with append access.** Anyone who can append to the store can produce an entry claiming `actor = "agent:alice"` with no way for a verifier to refute it using the audit crate alone.
2. **Hash linkage only prevents after-the-fact tampering.** A fresh chain assembled from forged entries passes `verify_chain` cleanly: seqs are monotonic, prev pointers match, zone IDs align.
3. **`event_types::AUDIT_FORK_DETECTED` handling is incomplete.** `verify_chain` detects `audit.fork_detected` (two entries at same seq with different ids) but provides no policy — a verifier cannot tell which fork is authoritative without out-of-band signer info.

## Why this is underspecified
The crate-level rustdoc (lib.rs:1-5) says "protocol-level types for audit chains, decision receipts, event filtering, and chain verification." It does not state that authenticity is out of scope and must be enforced by a wrapping layer. A reader (or agent) building against fcp-audit can plausibly conclude that `verify_chain` is a full trust root — it is not.

## Recommendation (choose one)
- **Option A (strong):** Add `issuer_kid: KeyId` + `signature: Ed25519Signature` fields to `AuditEntry`. Have `verify_chain` accept a `key_lookup: impl Fn(&KeyId) -> Option<Ed25519VerifyingKey>` and verify each entry. Requires depending on fcp-crypto.
- **Option B (docs + fork policy):** Add crate-level rustdoc explicitly stating: "AuditEntry authenticity is out of scope for this crate. Callers MUST wrap entries with issuer signatures before persisting; `verify_chain` only verifies integrity against edits, not authenticity against forgery." Add a `ForkPolicy` enum to `VerifyReport` so fork-resolution semantics are explicit.

Option B is the viable minimum; Option A is the principled fix if authenticity should live in the crate.

## Evidence
- crates/fcp-audit/src/lib.rs:152-185 (AuditEntry: no signature field)
- crates/fcp-audit/src/lib.rs:234-261 (computed_id: blake3 hash, not signature)
- crates/fcp-audit/src/lib.rs:927-1158 (verify_chain: no signer lookup)
- crates/fcp-audit/src/lib.rs:1-5 (crate docs do not disclose the gap)
- crates/fcp-audit/Cargo.toml:14-21 (no fcp-crypto dep confirming gap is architectural)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:47:37.678875Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T05:19:52.063018Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T05:19:51.453377Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2805,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-la0l1","author":"jemanuel","text":"Shipped in commit bef626cf. Added issuer_kid + signature optional fields to AuditEntry, signing_bytes/sign/verify_signature methods, and verify_chain_with_signers wrapper that enforces full authenticity. Three new error variants (SignerMissing/SignatureInvalid/UnknownIssuer). Six regression tests cover sign/verify roundtrip, wrong-key rejection, tamper detection, unsigned-entry rejection, multi-entry signed chain acceptance, mid-chain unsigned rejection, and unknown-issuer rejection. Crate-level docs and verify_chain rustdoc now explicitly disclose the authenticity vs integrity distinction. Backwards-compatible: serde-default Optional fields, existing verify_chain unchanged.","created_at":"2026-04-21T05:19:52Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lfedd","title":"fcp-mesh: authenticated symbol requests bypass proof-of-need amplification gate","description":"crates/fcp-mesh/src/symbol_request.rs:277-328
crates/fcp-mesh/src/admission.rs:913-931

Excerpt:
```
let base_limit = if is_authenticated { self.policy.max_authenticated_response } else { ... };
...
if max_response_symbols > self.policy.require_proof_of_need_above
    && !has_proof_of_need
    && !is_authenticated
```

Unused normative helper:
```
pub const fn check_amplification(..., is_authenticated: bool, has_proof_of_need: bool) -> Result<(), AdmissionError>
```

Finding: `validate_request` only requires proof-of-need for unauthenticated peers, so an authenticated requester can request a large response without any bounded missing-hint. The normative anti-amplification helper exists but is not wired into the symbol-request admission path.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:45:35.581795Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:05:33.650961Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:05:33.650619Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-lhl9m","title":"[REVIEW-P4][MEDIUM] fcp-crypto::HpkeSealedBox::from_bytes accepts unbounded ciphertext","description":"crates/fcp-crypto/src/hpke_seal.rs:69-78 only checks that the input is at least HPKE_ENC_SIZE + HPKE_TAG_SIZE, then clones the entire remainder into ciphertext with no upper bound. crates/fcp-crypto/src/hpke_seal.rs:214-237 then feeds sealed_box.ciphertext directly into hpke_open, which returns a newly allocated plaintext Vec. Bug scenario: a hostile peer or corrupted store record can hand a 100MB-plus sealed box to this public parser; from_bytes clones the whole ciphertext and the open path may allocate another full-sized plaintext buffer before the caller has any chance to enforce a limit, turning wrapped-zone-key or secret-share parsing into a memory-pressure DoS. Suggested fix: add a MAX_HPKE_SEALED_BOX_BYTES cap (or require callers to pass an expected plaintext/ciphertext bound) and reject oversized ciphertext before cloning or decrypting.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:33:08.351331Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:49:26.405866Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-lhl9m","title":"[REVIEW-P4][MEDIUM] fcp-crypto::HpkeSealedBox::from_bytes accepts unbounded ciphertext","description":"crates/fcp-crypto/src/hpke_seal.rs:69-78 only checks that the input is at least HPKE_ENC_SIZE + HPKE_TAG_SIZE, then clones the entire remainder into ciphertext with no upper bound. crates/fcp-crypto/src/hpke_seal.rs:214-237 then feeds sealed_box.ciphertext directly into hpke_open, which returns a newly allocated plaintext Vec. Bug scenario: a hostile peer or corrupted store record can hand a 100MB-plus sealed box to this public parser; from_bytes clones the whole ciphertext and the open path may allocate another full-sized plaintext buffer before the caller has any chance to enforce a limit, turning wrapped-zone-key or secret-share parsing into a memory-pressure DoS. Suggested fix: add a MAX_HPKE_SEALED_BOX_BYTES cap (or require callers to pass an expected plaintext/ciphertext bound) and reject oversized ciphertext before cloning or decrypting.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:33:08.351331Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:52:06.384017Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:52:06.383725Z","close_reason":"Capped HpkeSealedBox::from_bytes input at 64 KiB and reject oversized sealed boxes early","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lib","title":"fcp.zendesk: Customer Lookup","description":"# fcp.zendesk: Customer Lookup (FCP2)

## Goal
Enable agents to retrieve customer context safely:
- look up users/requesters
- fetch basic profile metadata
- list recent tickets for context

This is read-heavy but touches PII.

## Scope
- Search users by email/name/external id.
- Fetch user profile (minimize returned PII).
- List tickets for a user (pagination).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `zendesk.users.read`
- `zendesk.tickets.read`

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- PII hygiene:
  - never log emails/phone numbers
  - return only the minimal fields required for support workflows

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `zendesk.user.search`
  - `zendesk.user.get`
  - `zendesk.user.tickets.list`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - search pagination
  - redaction rules

## Acceptance criteria
- Customer context can be retrieved without leaking PII into logs.
","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:21:48.246203316Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-03T00:00:51.465463Z","closed_at":"2026-03-03T00:00:51.465448Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fcp2","tier3"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lib","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-pwff","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lib","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-pwff.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":538,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lib","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Audited: Customer lookup already implemented. Operation: zendesk.search_users. Covered by existing test suite.","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:00:51Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lj2","title":"fcp-oauth: Shared OAuth2/OAuth1 Authentication Library","description":"# fcp-oauth: Shared OAuth2/OAuth1 Authentication Library

## Overview
Unified OAuth library supporting OAuth 2.0 (authorization code, client credentials, PKCE) and OAuth 1.0a flows for connectors requiring user authorization.

## Supported Flows

### OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code (with PKCE)
- Authorization URL generation
- State parameter for CSRF protection
- PKCE challenge/verifier
- Token exchange
- Refresh token handling

### OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials
- Service-to-service authentication
- Token caching with TTL
- Automatic refresh

### OAuth 1.0a (Twitter legacy)
- Request token acquisition
- User authorization
- Access token exchange
- HMAC-SHA1 signature generation

## Token Management
- In-memory token storage (no disk!)
- Automatic refresh before expiry
- Token validation
- Secure token handling

## Provider Support
- Google (OAuth 2.0 + PKCE)
- GitHub (OAuth 2.0)
- Twitter/X (OAuth 1.0a + OAuth 2.0)
- Slack (OAuth 2.0)
- Notion (OAuth 2.0)
- Linear (OAuth 2.0)
- Custom providers via configuration

## Security
- State parameter validation
- PKCE enforcement where supported
- Secure redirect URI handling
- Token encryption at rest

## Dependencies
- reqwest, serde_json, chrono, base64
- fcp-core (for FCP integration)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-11T15:35:45.591389925Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T18:38:26.814607347Z","closed_at":"2026-01-11T18:38:26.814607347Z","close_reason":"Implemented fcp-oauth crate with OAuth 2.0 (authorization code + PKCE, client credentials), OAuth 1.0a, token management, and pre-configured providers. All 25 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-lobrq","title":"[BUG] capability-token golden vectors stale: principal_zone claim (key 3) added without vector regeneration","description":"Unmasked by 8kjnm fix (cod_4 / SunnyMoose, 2026-04-23): once crates/fcp-conformance/tests/serialization_roundtrip_conformance.rs compiles again, two tests fail because the three CapabilityTokenGoldenVector entries in crates/fcp-conformance/src/vectors/capability.rs encode 9-entry CWT claim maps but the current CapabilityToken serializer emits 10 entries — a new claim at positive key 3 (text \"z:work\") was added upstream without a golden regeneration.

**Failing tests** (both in the same test binary, both compile-clean, both runtime-failing):
- capability_token_cose_roundtrip_conformance (crates/fcp-conformance/src/vectors/capability.rs:319 iteration over load_all)
- serialization_conformance_matrix (crates/fcp-conformance/tests/serialization_roundtrip_conformance.rs:360)

**Root cause (git forensics):**
- 531b2a95 (fix(fcp-conformance): regenerate capability-token golden vectors (br-b6rtk)) set the current 9-entry vectors. expected_token_cbor shape: a9 {1:issuer, 4:nbf, 5:exp, -1:cap, -2:zone, -3:ops, -4:principal, -7:node, -16:schema_version}
- c828a3e7 (fix(fcp-core): add principal zone access floor) added a new claim at positive key 3 whose value is the string form of the principal's zone (e.g. \"z:work\"). No matching vector regen in that commit.
- All three vectors now serialize to a10 {1,3,4,5,-1,-2,-3,-4,-7,-16} — diff confirmed in test output bytes (vector 1: 03667a3a776f726b inserted; vector 2: 03697a3a70726976617465; vector 3: 03687a3a7075626c6963).

**Proposed fix** (one of):
(a) If the new key-3 claim is intended to be a permanent part of the canonical token: regenerate the three expected_claims_cbor + expected_token_cbor hex strings in crates/fcp-conformance/src/vectors/capability.rs, bump the docstring reference (\"Regenerated after c828a3e7 added principal_zone at CWT key 3\"), and re-sign to produce new sig tails. There's a CWT-builder test inside the module (all_vectors_verify at ~:313 / :319) that will emit the new bytes if you instrument a println.
(b) If the addition was a mistake or should be gated (optional field): fix the encoder to omit key 3 when principal_zone is derivable from the zone_id claim.

**DoD:**
- cargo test -p fcp-conformance --test serialization_roundtrip_conformance green (all 10 tests pass)
- cargo test -p fcp-conformance --test negative_path_conformance green (capability_golden_signature_single_byte_tamper_is_rejected depends on these vectors)
- docstring on each CapabilityTokenGoldenVector constructor mentions the regeneration commit

Cross-reference: unmasked during closure of flywheel_connectors-8kjnm.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:15:50.534504Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:22:29.291965Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:22:29.291371Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2825,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-lobrq","author":"jemanuel","text":"Resolved in commit 12667e02.

Regenerated expected_token_cbor for all three CapabilityTokenGoldenVector entries to reflect the 10-entry signed payload after c828a3e7 began synthesizing principal_zone at CWT key 3.

Key insight: principal_zone is a sign-time synthesis (like SCHEMA_VERSION at key -16), NOT a CwtClaims field. So expected_claims_cbor (raw CwtClaims::to_cbor() output) stays byte-identical; only expected_token_cbor needed regen.

Verified green:
- serialization_roundtrip_conformance: 10/10 (was 8/10)
- negative_path_conformance: 6/6 (depends on golden vectors for tamper-rejection tests)
- fcp-conformance --lib: 947/947

— cod_4 / SunnyMoose","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:22:28Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-uzamd","title":"[REVIEW-P5][MEDIUM] fcp-telemetry metrics helpers allow cardinality DoS","description":"crates/fcp-telemetry/src/metrics.rs:107-176 and 259-431 turn every caller-supplied label value into an owned String and emit it directly to the metrics backend. Public helpers like record_request_error(), record_event_emitted(), and record_event_dropped() accept unbounded connector, operation, error_type, event_type, reason, and zone strings with no normalization or cardinality cap. If those dimensions are derived from untrusted remote values, an attacker can vary them per request and force unbounded metric series growth plus repeated heap allocation. Real behavior should bucket or bound externally sourced labels before emission.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:42:38.796452Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:42:38.796452Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-v27h7","title":"[PERF] fcp-cbor::CanonicalSerializer::serialize: allocates and copies canonical body twice","description":"crates/fcp-cbor/src/lib.rs:208-219 and 331-352. CanonicalSerializer::serialize() builds buf with the schema prefix, then calls to_canonical_cbor(value) which allocates a second Vec, fully serializes the canonical body into it, and finally copies those bytes back into buf. For every hot-path schema-bound serialization this adds an extra O(payload_bytes) allocation and memcpy on top of the Value materialization/canonicalization already done inside write_canonical_cbor(). Proposed improvement: reserve once, append the schema hash, and call write_canonical_cbor(value, &mut buf) directly so the canonical body is written into the destination buffer without the intermediate Vec.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:49:14.536744Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:49:14.536744Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-v2uw9","title":"[FCP3/P5] Make placement, leases, and failover real in the mesh","description":"Background: the project is not mesh first until real cross node placement, coordinator exclusivity, checkpoint handoff, and failover work under evidence backed control. A speculative design or single node abstraction does not satisfy the architectural intent.

Scope: define and implement placement, lease and fencing, and resume semantics in fcp-mesh, then prove them on a narrow two node pilot. This phase must include fault injection, crate local unit coverage for planner and lease semantics, integration coverage for fencing and handoff, scripted end to end failover drills, and detailed placement and recovery logs that make user visible remediation and operator diagnosis obvious rather than mysterious. The phase ends when at least one connector family can be placed, failed over, resumed, and explained through the mesh native model.

Acceptance: at least one connector family can be placed, failed over, resumed, and debugged through the mesh native model with reproducible evidence bundles, transcripts, and detailed diagnostics that show users and operators exactly what happened before, during, and after failure.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:02.161063Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-02T21:54:25.040144Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T21:54:25.039927Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["failover","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","mesh","mesh-first","phase-5"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-v2uw9","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-0qtvu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:14.567043Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-v2uw9","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-9095b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:14.131087Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-v2uw9","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-bz431","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:14.822271Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-v2uw9","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ij290","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:13.914477Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-v2uw9","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-k88bi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:14.358560Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2052,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-v2uw9","author":"CobaltHarbor","text":"This phase is the credibility checkpoint. If the system cannot survive node movement, fence duplicate executors, and explain why a placement happened, then it is still host first regardless of crate names or documentation language.","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:02Z"}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-v3wrz","title":"[REVIEW-P5][MEDIUM] fcp-webhook::check_replay and record_event race duplicate delivery","description":"crates/fcp-webhook/src/handler.rs:178-205 exposes check_replay() as a read-side duplicate check and 211-223 exposes record_event() as a separate write. Because the two operations are public and non-atomic, two concurrent deliveries of the same event_id can both pass check_replay() before either records, then both process the same event. claim_event() at 230-262 fixes this, but the split API still invites callers into an exactly-once race on untrusted webhook input. Real behavior should deprecate or harden the split pair so replay detection and claiming happen atomically.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:42:21.686186Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:49:15.158577Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-v3wrz","title":"[REVIEW-P5][MEDIUM] fcp-webhook::check_replay and record_event race duplicate delivery","description":"crates/fcp-webhook/src/handler.rs:178-205 exposes check_replay() as a read-side duplicate check and 211-223 exposes record_event() as a separate write. Because the two operations are public and non-atomic, two concurrent deliveries of the same event_id can both pass check_replay() before either records, then both process the same event. claim_event() at 230-262 fixes this, but the split API still invites callers into an exactly-once race on untrusted webhook input. Real behavior should deprecate or harden the split pair so replay detection and claiming happen atomically.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:42:21.686186Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:53:42.267650Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:53:42.266850Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2834,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-v3wrz","author":"jemanuel","text":"Resolved in commit c5f532c2.

Fix: #[deprecated] on both check_replay and record_event (the racy split pair), with docstrings pointing callers to claim_event (the atomic check-and-insert under a single write lock acquisition). All call sites are test-only — no production migration needed. Added #[allow(deprecated)] at module scope on the 3 test files that retain coverage of the legacy split pair so the regression surface isn't lost. Strengthened claim_event docstring to name it as the only correct path for exactly-once on untrusted webhook input.

New regression: claim_event_is_atomic_under_concurrent_deliveries spawns 16 OS threads racing to claim_event(same_id) and asserts exactly one Ok + THREADS-1 ReplayDetected via AtomicUsize counters — pins the atomicity property that motivated the deprecation.

Verified: cargo test -p fcp-webhook 574/574 pass (466 lib + 102 integration + 6 no_mock); cargo check --all-targets shows zero v3wrz deprecation leaks (only pre-existing zp1xx).

— cod_4 / SunnyMoose","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:53:41Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-v6w","title":"Study: Clawdbot Discord Connector Implementation","description":"# Study: Clawdbot Discord Connector Implementation


## Goal
Study Clawdbot Discord connector implementation to extract edge cases, performance pitfalls, and security lessons for FCP2.

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints`.
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Objective

Research and document the existing clawdbot Discord connector implementation to extract patterns, learnings, and reusable components for building FCP connectors.

## Research Areas

### 1. Architecture Overview
- [ ] Overall connector structure
- [ ] Module organization
- [ ] Dependency graph
- [ ] Configuration management
- [ ] Entry point and initialization

### 2. Discord API Integration
- [ ] Gateway connection handling
- [ ] REST API usage patterns
- [ ] Rate limiting implementation
- [ ] Retry logic and backoff
- [ ] Error handling strategies

### 3. Event Handling
- [ ] Event subscription model
- [ ] Event parsing and validation
- [ ] Event dispatching mechanism
- [ ] Event filtering capabilities
- [ ] Async event processing

### 4. Authentication & Security
- [ ] Token management
- [ ] Permission handling
- [ ] Credential storage patterns
- [ ] Security best practices used

### 5. Message Processing
- [ ] Message parsing
- [ ] Command extraction
- [ ] Response formatting
- [ ] Attachment handling
- [ ] Embed construction

### 6. State Management
- [ ] Guild/server state tracking
- [ ] User session management
- [ ] Cache strategies
- [ ] Persistence patterns

### 7. Testing Approach
- [ ] Unit test patterns
- [ ] Integration test setup
- [ ] Mock strategies for Discord API
- [ ] Test coverage areas

## Deliverables

1. **Architecture Diagram** - Visual representation of connector structure
2. **Pattern Catalog** - Documented patterns that can be reused
3. **Anti-Pattern Notes** - What to avoid based on learnings
4. **Reusable Components List** - Code/patterns that can be extracted
5. **Recommendations** - Suggestions for FCP connector design

## Questions to Answer

- How does clawdbot handle Discord's rate limits?
- What reconnection strategy is used for the gateway?
- How are slash commands registered and handled?
- How is state synchronized across restarts?
- What logging/observability patterns are used?

## Resources

- Clawdbot repository
- Discord.js/Discord.py documentation (for comparison)
- Discord API documentation
- Existing connector documentation

## Time Estimate

- Research: 4-6 hours
- Documentation: 2-3 hours
- Total: 6-9 hours

## Acceptance Criteria
- Runs deterministically in CI (no flake due to timing/network).
- Uses mocks only (no real external network).
- Emits structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35` with stable `test_name` identifiers.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-vc5","title":"fcp.jira: Automation rule management","description":"# fcp.jira: Automation rule management (FCP2)

## Goal
Support Jira Automation ("Automation for Jira") rule management for organizations that want:
- agent-assisted creation of automation rules
- safe enable/disable workflows
- auditing of automation changes

Automation configuration is **admin-level** and can have broad blast radius. This bead is explicitly treated as *dangerous*.

## Scope
### Read
- List automation rules (project-scoped / global-scoped where allowed).
- Get rule definition + status (enabled/disabled).

### Write (dangerous; approval-gated)
- Create rule (from a constrained schema).
- Update rule.
- Enable/disable rule.
- Delete rule.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `jira.automation.read`
- `jira.automation.write` (dangerous)
- `jira.admin` (dangerous; optional umbrella)

Policy guidance:
- Require ApprovalToken for any write.
- Include a two-person rule if the org policy requires it.

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Receipts + audit events for all writes with:
  - rule id
  - project id
  - diff summary (structural; no secrets)

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `jira.automation.rule.list`
  - `jira.automation.rule.get`
  - `jira.automation.rule.create`
  - `jira.automation.rule.update`
  - `jira.automation.rule.enable`
  - `jira.automation.rule.disable`
  - `jira.automation.rule.delete`
- A constrained rule representation:
  - avoid arbitrary script injection
  - prefer templates + parameterization

## Test strategy (MUST)
### Unit/integration (mock-only)
- Rule CRUD happy paths.
- Permission denied.
- Validation failures (unsafe actions rejected).
- Idempotency: repeated enable/disable does not drift.

### Compliance
- Ensure all write ops require appropriate capabilities + approval.
- Ensure audit evidence includes a stable structural hash of the rule definition.

## Acceptance criteria
- Connector can manage automation rules without enabling arbitrary code execution.
- All writes are approval-gated and produce receipts/audit events.

Write ops are Dangerous tier with approval gating. 58 new tests (12 type tests + 12 client wiremock tests + 34 connector tests). Total connector: 23 ops, 265 tests, all clippy clean. Committed as 6fca8cfc.","created_at":"2026-03-09T07:27:39Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ve470","title":"Resolve scaffolded-only connectors: implement or honestly demote to stub status","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:12:53.319505Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-25T05:26:15.461888Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T05:26:15.461632Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","hardening","scaffolds"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ve470","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1z643","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:13:09.900201Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1959,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ve470","author":"jemanuel","text":"EPIC: Scaffolded-Only Connectors

Six connectors declare operations in their manifest and handle invoke() in their connector.rs, but every operation returns:
  const UNIMPLEMENTED_REASON_CODE: &str = "invoke_surface_unimplemented";
  const UNIMPLEMENTED_MESSAGE: &str = "This connector scaffold only declares planned operations. Live invoke support is not implemented yet.";

These are: firecrawl, huggingface, perplexity-search, tlon, zalo, zalouser

This is problematic because:
1. fwc list --offline shows these as available connectors
2. fwc ops shows their declared operations
3. An agent could attempt to invoke them and get a confusing error
4. They inflate the connector count (README says '136 connector crates' but 6 are non-functional)

RESOLUTION OPTIONS:
A) Implement: Build real API client, types, and operations. Effort varies by connector.
B) Honest stub: Add a 'status = stub' field to manifest.toml, make fwc list show it, make doctor() report it clearly.
C) Remove from workspace: Take them out of Cargo.toml workspace members until ready.

RECOMMENDATION: Option B for immediate transparency, then Option A when there's demand.

The 6 connectors break into natural groups:
- firecrawl, huggingface, perplexity-search: AI/search tools with well-documented REST APIs. Implementation is straightforward.
- tlon: Urbit network connector. Niche platform with limited documentation. May never have demand.
- zalo, zalouser: Vietnamese messaging platform. Limited English documentation, restricted API access outside Vietnam.","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:13:10Z"}]}
